news.summary: On May 14, 2025, in Washington, the Association of Black Cardiologists (ABC) praised the Illinois State Legislature and Senator Napoleon Harris III for their efforts in promoting vital legislation. The proposed law aims to mandate insurance providers to include coverage for essential peripheral artery medical treatments. The ABC has applauded this move as a significant step forward in ensuring access to necessary healthcare services for individuals affected by peripheral artery issues.
news.headline: Association of Black Cardiologists praises Illinois bill mandating insurance to cover Peripheral Artery Disease screening; supported by Senator Harris III.

news.summary: Westerville, Ohio-based Lancaster Colony Corporation (Nasdaq: LANC) issued a statement revealing its Board of Directors' decision to declare a quarterly cash dividend of 95 cents per common share. This dividend is set to be paid on June 30, 2025, to shareholders on record as of June 6, 2025. The latest amount of 95 cents per share represents a continuation of the increased dividend level established six months earlier, denoting the company's 62nd consecutive year of raising regular cash dividends. Lancaster Colony stands as one of merely 12 U.S. companies with such an impressive track record of consistent dividend growth.
news.headline: Lancaster Colony raises cash dividend to 95 cents per share, set to pay out on June 30, 2025; marks 62nd year of increases.

news.summary: A Houston startup has successfully demonstrated a revolutionary propulsion technology, setting the stage for hypersonic vehicles to be launched from regular runways. Venus Aerospace, the company behind this breakthrough, revealed the news on May 14, 2025. This development marks a significant advancement in the field of high-speed flight and has the potential to transform the aerospace industry. The startup's achievement showcases the innovative capabilities of the technology sector in Houston and presents new opportunities for the future of air travel.
news.headline: Houston startup Venus Aerospace completes historic U.S. hypersonic engine flight test, revolutionizing propulsion technology.

news.summary: In CAESAREA, Israel, there has been a significant development in the deployment of AI applications and agents by enterprise and service providers. With a sharp focus on cost-effective performance, Rashid Attar, the senior vice president of engineering at Qualcomm Technologies, Inc., emphasized the rapid pace at which these technologies are being implemented. By combining the Qualcomm Cloud AI 100 Ultra accelerators with NeuReality's AI-CPU architecture, users can now attain heightened levels of both cost efficiency and AI performance. This innovation allows users to achieve these results without sacrificing ease of use.
news.headline: NeuReality aims to slash AI costs by integrating Qualcomm Cloud AI 100 Ultra accelerators with its AI-CPU architecture.

This initiative aims to address health disparities and improve access to healthcare services for residents in need. Stay tuned for more updates on this program.

news.summary: In a recent report, it was revealed that the unemployment rate in the United States dropped to 4.6% in the month of February, the lowest it has been in over two years. This decrease in unemployment was accompanied by a significant increase in job creation, with the economy adding 678,000 jobs during the same month. Additionally, the average hourly earnings for workers rose by 5.1% compared to the previous year, indicating positive growth in wages. These latest economic indicators suggest a strong recovery in the labor market and point towards a promising outlook for the future.
news.headline: Annual General Meeting results for Nilörngruppen AB: Key stats revealed with record revenue of $10 million and profit growth of 15%.

This initiative is aimed at providing support for nurses, enhancing workforce sustainability, and improving care quality for communities with high needs.

news.summary: Arcos Dorados Holdings Inc. (NYSE: ARCO) saw a decline in its shares following the release of first-quarter results. The company, acting as McDonald's master franchise in 20 countries and territories in Latin America and the Caribbean, reported earnings per share of 7 cents, falling short of the expected 13 cents. Despite this, quarterly sales remained steady at $1.08 billion, surpassing the analyst consensus of $1.05 billion. Total revenues experienced a 14.1% increase in constant currency, driven by a notable 11.1% rise in systemwide comparable sales. Digital sales also showed growth, increasing by 6.3% during the period with the support of close to 19 million monthly average users.
news.headline: Arcos Dorados stock falls after Q1 results: EPS 7 cents (below Street view 13 cents), sales $1.08B (flat y-o-y), digital sales up 6.3%.

news.summary: In the first quarter of 2025, Exelixis Inc. (NASDAQ: EXEL) reported adjusted earnings of 62 cents per share, a significant increase from the 17 cents per share reported a year earlier, surpassing the expected 36 cents per share. The company's quarterly sales reached $555.45 million, exceeding the anticipated $498.18 million. The total revenues included net product revenues amounting to $513.3 million compared to $378.5 million in the same period in 2024. Following the positive performance of CABOMETYX in the first quarter, Exelixis has raised its full-year financial guidance for 2025 by $100 million for net product revenues and total revenues, as stated by Michael M. Morrissey, the president and CEO. The FDA has also approved Exelixis' lead cancer drug Cabometyx for expanded use in Neuroendocrine Tumors. For further details, the complete article is accessible on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Exelixis Inc.'s Q1 2025 earnings soar to 62 cents/share, beating consensus of 36 cents, with $555.45 million in sales.CEO raises 2025 guidance by $100 million.

news.summary: VanEck has recently introduced a new actively managed ETF called the VanEck Onchain Economy ETF (NODE) listed on the Cboe exchange, providing exposure to companies in the blockchain ecosystem. The NODE ETF will invest in stocks and financial instruments related to the digital economy, including crypto miners, exchanges, and infrastructure providers. It will not directly hold any cryptocurrencies but may invest in crypto-related financial instruments. The fund will continuously update its investable universe as new companies enter, and VanEck aims to ensure responsible exposure to Bitcoin. Additionally, VanEck launched another ETF in April, the VanEck Digital Transformation ETF (DAPP), which has $185 million in net assets. Asset managers like VanEck are seeking SEC approval for numerous crypto ETF listings following a relaxation in regulatory stance under the new US administration. VanEck also requested SEC approval to list an ETF holding the BNB Chain native token. This move aligns with a trend of increasing interest in crypto ETFs, with over 70 filings submitted, driven by the evolving regulatory landscape.
news.headline: VanEck launches Onchain Economy ETF (NODE) aiming to offer exposure to digital economy; also filed for another ETF.

news.summary: Summer Mersinger has joined the Blockchain Association amid reports suggesting the CFTC will emerge as the primary regulatory authority for the industry.
news.headline: Former CFTC Commissioner Summer Mersinger chosen to head leading crypto lobbying firm amid the agency’s shift towards industry regulation.

news.summary: In a press release dated May 14, 2025, Critical One Energy Inc. (formerly Madison Metals Inc.), trading under the tickers CSE: CRTL, OTCQB: MMTLF, and FSE: 4EF0, revealed a significant discovery at the Howells Lake Antimony-Gold Project. The Company uncovered noteworthy amounts of antimony, with a specific drill hole, MC-V3-79-22, showcasing levels as high as 75% antimony over 8.35 meters within a core interval measuring 5.37% antimony. Additionally, gold assays were found in the extensive historical records of the project. This discovery marks a pivotal moment for Critical One Energy Inc. as it continues its exploration and development efforts.
news.headline: Critical One Energy Inc. discovers up to 75% antimony in drill hole MC-V3-79-22 at Howells Lake Project.

news.summary: On May 14, 2025, Geneva and New York are hosting a groundbreaking event to support the rights and dignity of all individuals. This event marks the inaugural Ring the Bell for LGBTIQ+ Equality initiative, where stock exchanges globally are uniting this month. The event is aimed to promote equality for the LGBTQ+ community and advocate for their rights. This initiative highlights the importance of inclusivity and diversity on a global scale.
news.headline: Global stock exchanges unite to support LGBTIQ+ inclusion with Ring the Bell for Equality initiative; May 2025 launch.

news.summary: U.S. tech stocks saw a surge on Wednesday, continuing their strong performance for the week, driven by increased investor confidence following positive U.S.-China trade talks. The Nasdaq 100 rose by 0.5% in New York, hitting levels not seen since late February. Chipmakers like Nvidia and AMD saw significant gains, with Nvidia rising by 4% and AMD by 5%. Expectations of substantial investments from Gulf nations into U.S. tech and semiconductor industries, alongside new economic agreements with Middle Eastern countries signed by President Trump, also contributed to the momentum. While the Nasdaq performed well, the S&P 500 stagnated, and the Dow Jones Industrial Average and Russell 2000 saw slight declines, indicating a preference for high-growth sectors. Clean energy and solar stocks particularly excelled, building on previous gains. Investors responded positively to the news of revised clean energy tax credits. This story can be found on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Tech stocks, especially AMD, rally 5% as Nasdaq 100 rises 0.5%, led by chipmakers. U.S.-China trade talk progress drives investor risk appetite. Gulf countries looking to invest in U.S. semiconductor sector, with the S&P 500 stalling. Clean energy and solar stocks continue to outperform. Trump in talks with Middle Eastern countries for new economic agreements. Dow Jones and Russell 2000 slightly down.
